https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/oom/+/101749/
please use apps/v1 version
awwww ingress enabled by default awesome!:)

Please use secrets to access this.
We have also a new secret template which you can use.
Password should be passed as a secret
Don't hardcode postgres password use common secret template. Let me know if you need help with this
secret
secret

Possibly I am doing something wrong, but with the following in values.yaml:
service:
    type: ClusterIP
    name: dcaemod-designtool
    ports:
    - name: http
      port: 8080

I get the following k8s spec:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:

  name: dcaemod-designtool
  namespace: onap
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: dcaemod-designtool
    helm.sh/chart: dcaemod-designtool-6.0.0
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: testmod
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Tiller
spec:
  ports:

    - port: 8080
      targetPort: http
      name: http
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: dcaemod-designtool
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: testmod

The targetPort should be a port number, not a copy of the port name.   The template should probably not generate the targetPort at all.  Rather, if it's desired to have the port and targetPort be different, the values.yaml should supply a targetPort value.   Otherwise, targetPort can be omitted, and k8s will default it to the same value as port.

When I attempt to deploy the chart, I get the following error:
Error: release testmod failed: Service "dcaemod-distributor-api" is invalid: spec.ports: Required value

See _service.tpl line 68 for the targetPort setting.

Also I note that the selectors assume labeling on the target deployment that follow a different convention from what we have used in the past.  

At the head of the master branch, I don't see any project using this template yet.
I propose not to use the service template in the Frankfurt release, unless there is a simple fix I can make to my values.yaml or service.yaml file.
Addressed in patchset #3
Done in patchset 4
Done in patchset 4
Done in patchset 4

Have you tested that it's actually a bug and sth is not working?
According to kubernetes doc:
https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.17/#serviceport-v1-core
This field can be either a number or a name

Yes, I tried installing using the charts generated with the service template, and reported the error in my previous comment.   
Error: release testmod failed: Service "dcaemod-distributor-api" is invalid: spec.ports: Required value

It looks to me like "ports" is present in the spec.  There are some extraneous blank lines in the output, but I don't think those are the issue. 

If I supply the rendered output directly to kubernetes (with kubectl apply or create), it creates a service.

Yes, I need help!

I did not find any examples of other projects using the common postgres charts with templates. If you can point me to one, that might be sufficient.

I'm not able to retrieve the postgres secret from the environment variable.

I followed the pattern in the common postgres _deployment.tpl template to try to retrieve the postgres root password in my deployment, like this:
          env:
            - name: PG_ROOT_PW
              {{- include "common.secret.envFromSecret" (dict "global" . "uid" (include "common.postgres.secret.rootPassUID" .) "key" "password") | indent 14 }}
           
When this is rendered, the secretKeyRef.name field is empty:
          env:
            - name: PG_ROOT_PW              
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: 
                  key: password

I suspect I need to pass something other than "." as the value for "global", but I don't know what it is. Or possibly I need to put something else in my deployment to make the resolution of the secret name work properly.   Please let me know.

Krzysztof - Jack informed there is an issue/bug to use the new template/generated password for postgres (https://jira.onap.org/browse/OOM-2317). 

As we are litterally few days from M4, I would suggest deferring work to refactor current chart for new templates part of subsequent delivery, that way we are not blocked from delivering MOD for Frankfurt. We are currently working to release the dependent MOD images - once done, the verify job should succeed. Any pending review/comments from security standpoint could be tracked as separate jira and addressed post M4.

The previous comment was based on letting the postgres chart create the secrets, and having the client application use those secrets to set the appropriate environment variables.

The closest example I could find was the pending review for dmaap-dr's use of mariadb-galera, where the application creates the secret (allowing the password to be generated) and configures postgres to use that secret as an "external secret".   I am now taking the same approach with postgres for dcaemod-onboarding-api.

Unfortunately, the postgres container has a problem with certain special characters in the password, due to the way it converts the environment variable containing the password into the SQL instruction that actually sets the password.  Our password generation does not avoid these characters.   I have raised OOM-2317 to track this.

For R6, I propose continuing to set the database passwords explicitly in values.yaml.

Hi, 
"The targetPort should be a port number, not a copy of the port name": it can also be the name of the port of the container, which makes it a lot more error prone IMHO.
the weird thing is that https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/102169/2/kubernetes/dcaemod/components/dcaemod-designtool/templates/deployment.yaml doesn't have container ports and maybe that's the issue no?
If you're OK, I can make a proposition on designtool to showcase how to do it

a lot LESS error prone sorry.
"linter" tools push actually to do that (https://github.com/derailed/popeye)
Yes, it is OK to make a change to designtool.
This should be "value:".  With "values", PG_DB_NAME is defined in the container but set to empty.  Will fix in my next submission.
You're right. Thank you for catching this

I just want to make sure that you understand how this common secret template works.

1) if config.dashboardPassword is set it will be put in the secret and used for every deployment. User could obviously change it but it's a manual acction

2) if config.dashboardPassword is not set at all then common secret template will generate a new password for every deployment (or for every masterPassword) to be more precise.

So is option #1 your intention?

I understand how the template works.  I intended option #1.  

This password is a placeholder--it isn't used. So it can be the same in every deployment. It would be used to authenticate to the inventory API, but the inventory API doesn't require authentication.  If the inventory API were to do authentication, then there would need to be a shared secret.  Either inventory would need to use the secret created here, or the runtime API would need to use a secret created by inventory.  If the inventory API were to do authentication/authorization via AAF, then it gets more complicated. In any of these cases, there would need to be changes to the Helm charts.

Since the password doesn't matter, I will change this to make it randomly-generated. But the change is meaningless--it doesn't impact security, and it doesn't change anything about deployment.
